source: Seventy-nine-year-old woman from home, lives alone, brought in by her daughter after a mechanical fall. She tripped over the edge of a rug, fell onto her right hip, no head strike, no loss of consciousness. She's got history of osteoporosis and well-controlled hypertension on lisinopril. Complaining of right hip pain, can't bear weight. X-ray shows a non-displaced right femoral neck fracture. Neuro intact, she's alert and oriented times three. No other injuries. Ortho's been consulted, they're planning surgical fixation tomorrow. Admitting to medicine for medical optimization, keeping her NPO after midnight, pain control with acetaminophen and low-dose oxycodone, DVT prophylaxis with enoxaparin, holding the lisinopril for now. Continue her home calcium and vitamin D.
reference:
HPI: 79F who lives alone presents after a mechanical fall — tripped on a rug, landed on the right hip. No head strike, no LOC. PMH osteoporosis, hypertension (lisinopril). Reports right hip pain, unable to bear weight.

Exam: Alert and oriented x3, neurologically intact. No other injuries.

Data: X-ray — non-displaced right femoral neck fracture.

Assessment/Plan:
# Right femoral neck fracture (non-displaced) — Ortho consulted, surgical fixation planned tomorrow. NPO after midnight. Pain control: acetaminophen + low-dose oxycodone. DVT prophylaxis: enoxaparin.
# Hypertension — hold lisinopril perioperatively.
# Osteoporosis — continue home calcium and vitamin D.
Admit to medicine for perioperative optimization.
